pages.title: About - Neil Coyle
pages.meta_description: Keeping my promises to Bermondsey and Old Southwark
pages.text_excerpt: About - Neil Coyle Skip to main content Home About Blog Testimonials Parliamentary Activity Directory of Support In the media Contact & Surgeries Privacy statement More Menu About Neil Coyle MP with his daughter Esme and his dog Ruby at the beach Personal Born in Luton, December 1978. One of six children in total (one adopted). Attended Wenlock, Ashcroft and Bedford schools and Hull University. Lived in China 2001-03. Lives in Walworth, SE17 with wife, Sarah (married 2014), and daughter Esme. Previously lived in Grange, Cathedrals and Newington wards in SE1. Sarah is a Landscape Architect who has lived and worked in Southwark since 2005. UK career Worked at Government equality commission, 2003-07, finishing as Policy Manager for health and social care. Head of Policy at National Centre for Independent Living, 2007-09 Director of Policy at Disability Alliance 2009-12 Director of Policy and Campaigns at Disability Rights UK, 2012-13 Political roles Newington ward Councillor, London Borough of Southwark, 2010-16 Chair of Health and Adult Social Care scrutiny committee 2010-11 Vice-chair of the Walworth Community Council 2010-11 Deputy Cabinet member, London Borough of Southwark 2011-13 Labour parliamentary candidate for Bermondsey and Old Southwark, 2013-15 Deputy Mayor of Southwark, 2014-15 Labour MP for Bermondsey and Old Southwark May 2015-Present Work and Pensions Select Committee, 2016-2024 Foreign Affairs Committee, 2020-2024 Contributing locally I was Southwark Council's Deputy Cabinet Member for Welfare, helping shape the council's policies and reaction to Simon Hughes' Government cuts to local disabled people and families on low incomes. I helped establish a Hardship Fund to support our most disadvantaged friends and neighbours. I also helped found the Walworth Society - ensuring local people get a greater voice in planning and regeneration issues in particular, but also providing greater voice and focus at local level. I am also a Trustee for the North Southwark Environment Trust - helping fund local educational and environmental projects across the north of Southwark, including the Alf Salter replacement statue campaign. I have also worked to establish new tenants and residents' associations to ensure a stronger, local voice. Contact details Neil Coyle, Jamaica Road, London, UK View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Robert Smeath on behalf of Neil Coyle, both of 145 Jamaica Road, SE16 4SH ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Help Vimal Pandya - Neil Coyle
pages.meta_description: Keeping my promises to Bermondsey and Old Southwark
pages.text_excerpt: Help Vimal Pandya - Neil Coyle Skip to main content Home About Blog Testimonials Parliamentary Activity Directory of Support In the media Contact & Surgeries Privacy statement More Menu 20th May, 2025 Help Vimal Pandya Vimal Pandya after winning an award at Southwark Cathedral Link to support: here Help Vimal Pandya Vimal has been a huge asset to Rotherhithe and Southwark more widely. His efforts during the covid lockdowns meant people (often older or disabled people) who couldn’t go out had their prescriptions and shopping delivered at home. His amazing efforts helping hundreds of people earned him recognition from Queen Elizabeth II. When the Home Office told Vimal to stop working, he expanded his volunteering – even helping in my local Bermondsey office supporting other people in need. He has been instrumental in establishing the Rotherhithe Community Kitchen which provides hot food to hundreds of people every week across Southwark. He recently received additional recognition for his voluntary work in our community at Southwark Cathedral. But Vimal needs your help. Under existing Home Office rules, Vimal was detained and could face deportation. A judicial review of the process is underway and I am intervening to examine whether Vimal’s truly exceptional contribution has been recognised and whether there are other means of ensuring he can continue making such a positive contribution in Southwark. A part of the process, it is important people say what Vimal means to you and what would happen if he was not able to continue contributing here. Please make it as personal as possible and focus on the positive difference Vimal has made to you and your family or circumstances. Please complete the form saying what Vimal means to you and why he needs to stay. Your contribution could help ensure Vimal is able to keep making such a positive contribution here. Thank you in advance for your kind words – and please let me know if you have any questions about the process or if you’d like to help Vimal further. Link to support: here Contact details Neil Coyle, Jamaica Road, London, UK View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Robert Smeath on behalf of Neil Coyle, both of 145 Jamaica Road, SE16 4SH ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
